title: Live Mashup Techniques
description: Blend two tracks simultaneously at equal volume to create a mashup — their basslines, melodies, and rhythms merge into something new.

requiredTracks.characteristics: harmonically compatible tracks — same or adjacent Camelot keys

steps.instruction: Watch: both decks play at 80% volume simultaneously — this is the mashup zone. Both tracks contribute equally.
Watch: Deck A owns the bass (Deck B's LOW EQ cut). Deck B contributes the melody and highs. Separation creates clarity.
Watch: the roles swap — Deck B takes the bass, Deck A keeps the melody. The mashup shifts character.

phases.instruction: A live mashup is about control through EQ. Both tracks play simultaneously but you decide what each contributes. Track A owns the low end. Track B owns the melody. Then you swap, or blend, or sculpt. The listener hears something neither track could produce alone.

steps.instruction: Beatmatch both decks precisely — within 0.5 BPM. For a mashup, pitch drift will ruin the blend.
Cut Deck B's LOW EQ to -24 dB. Deck B enters without bass — only melody and highs.
Phase-align Deck B to Deck A. Both must be on bar 1 of a phrase simultaneously.
Bring Deck B's volume up to 80%. Now both tracks play — Deck A with full EQ, Deck B with no bass. Listen to the mashup.
Let it play for 8 bars. Listen for harmonic compatibility — do the melodies complement or clash?
Now sculpt the blend. Try giving Deck B's MID more presence — boost it slightly to hear its melody clearer.
Execute the bass swap — cut Deck A's LOW EQ and restore Deck B's LOW EQ at a phrase boundary.
Restore Deck B's LOW EQ to center. The bass has transferred. The mashup shifts character — a new combination.
